page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, yielding a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, providing 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction sets. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core testing placing in the top 25 percent. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth reaching 57.86 GB/sec uncached read, and Geekbench scores fall within the average range. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM prompt processing show weaker performance, placing in the bottom 25 percent.

Given its high memory density relative to compute resources, the n2d-highmem-8 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. It is designed for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, data buffering, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 8 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, utilizing a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core, supported by a 32 MB L3 cache and instruction sets such as AVX and AES. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, while multi-core execution, memory bandwidth, and web serving throughput remain average. Conversely, multi-threaded decompression and medium-model LLM prompt processing exhibit weaker performance. This hardware profile provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that require high RAM-to-vCPU ratios without demanding extreme parallel compute. It is suitable for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual machine featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.

Dedicated CPU allocation with 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for in-memory databases and caching workloads
